Follow publication Press enter or click to view image in full size ![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*nH14QQ-fS5fF091Ri85ZRA.png) # How Claude’s New Generative UI Works (And How to Build It Yourself) [![Saurabh Singh](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/1*KBwFkYasd9S1RWMZDkh1cA.jpeg)](https://medium.com/@sausi?source=post_page---byline--99b3170c346b---------------------------------------) [Saurabh Singh](https://medium.com/@sausi?source=post_page---byline--99b3170c346b---------------------------------------) Follow 6 min read · 19 hours ago 7 [Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3D99b3170c346b&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fai.plainenglish.io%2Fhow-claudes-new-generative-ui-works-and-how-to-build-it-yourself-99b3170c346b&source=---header_actions--99b3170c346b---------------------post_audio_button------------------) Share ## AI Just Crossed Another Interface Boundary For years, AI systems have mostly generated **text**. Then they started generating **images, code, and videos**. But something interesting happened recently. Claude introduced something called **Generative UI** — where the AI doesn’t just describe an interface… It **builds the interface itself**. Not screenshots. Not HTML snippets inside a chat response. A **live interface** that appears and grows on screen **while the model is still generating it**. Charts animate into existence. Widgets become interactive. Buttons can send data back to the AI. All of it happens **in real time**. After digging into the architecture and building a working implementation myself, I realized something important: > _Generative UI isn’t just “AI writing HTML”._ It’s a completely different **architecture for how AI and interfaces interact**. This article breaks down **exactly how it works** and how you can **build it yourself**. ## Full Implementation If you’d like to explore the implementation discussed in this article: **Claude Demo Video** **GitHub Repo** [https://github.com/sausi-7/generative-ui-demo](https://github.com/sausi-7/generative-ui-demo) [**LinkedIn Post**](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/iamsausi_today-anthropic-released-generative-ui-for-activity-7438248839700070401-nwB8?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAADF9B2MBKXnGjwRkqu__nMB47VOJK0GsSoY) The entire system described here is built in **~800 lines of code** using: - FastAPI - Claude Tool Use - Server-Sent Events - Morphdom - Streaming partial JSON parsing Let’s break down the architecture step by step. ## The Question That Changes Everything Imagine you go to a restaurant and order a custom dish. In one version of this restaurant, the chef writes down the recipe on a card and hands it to you. You go home and cook it yourself. In another version, the chef cooks the meal **right in front of you**, and you watch each ingredient appear on the plate in real time until the dish is complete. Both approaches produce food. But they are **fundamentally different experiences**. And they represent two completely different **architectures**. This is exactly the difference between: **“Claude returns HTML that you render”** and **Generative UI.** ## Part 1: The Naive Approach Most developers initially try something like this. User asks: > _“Give me a bar chart of monthly sales.”_ Claude responds with HTML inside text: ```
``` Then the application renders it like this: ``` document.getElementById("output").innerHTML = response.text ``` It works… at first. But it quickly falls apart. ## Problem 1 — HTML Mixed With Text Claude outputs **explanations and HTML together**. Your app must parse the response and somehow separate: - explanation text - UI artifact This quickly becomes fragile. ## Problem 2 — Scripts Don’t Execute Browsers **ignore script tags inserted via innerHTML**. That means: - chart libraries never initialize - event handlers don’t attach - widgets become dead UI ## Problem 3 — No Streaming Users must wait for the **entire response** before anything renders. A 400-line HTML widget may take **several seconds** to appear. ## Problem 4 — No Shared Design System Claude guesses random colors like: ``` background: blue ``` These rarely match your app’s theme. ## Problem 5 — No Interactivity Loop If a user clicks something inside the widget, the AI never knows. The interface becomes a **static artifact** instead of a living system. Generative UI solves all five of these problems. Let’s see how. ## Part 2: Architecture Overview The system is composed of **three main layers**. ``` Browser │ ├─ Chat panel ├─ Widget panel │ ▼ FastAPI Server │ ▼ Claude API ``` The browser receives a **stream of events** containing: - text updates - widget fragments - final rendered UI The server acts as the **orchestrator**, intercepting tool calls and streaming updates. Claude generates both **text and UI fragments**. ## Part 3: Tool Calls as the Rendering Primitive The key insight is this: In Generative UI, **UI is not generated as text**. Instead, it is generated as a **structured tool call**. Claude outputs two streams simultaneously: **Text Stream** Explanation for the user. **Tool Call Stream** Structured data that contains the UI. Example tool call: ``` show_widget({ title: "compound_interest_calculator", widget_code: "
...
" }) ``` This means the application **never needs to parse HTML out of text**. The UI artifact already lives in a **separate structured channel**. ## Part 4: Streaming Widgets with Partial JSON Parsing Claude streams tool arguments **token by token**. That means the JSON arrives **incomplete**. Example partial stream: ``` {"widget_code": "

## Özet

This skill crafts distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality for websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, posters, and full web applications. Use it when asking to build or restyle web UI, create reusable components, or generate polished code and assets. Features include responsive layouts, accessible ARIA-compliant markup, modular CSS and CSS-in-JS patterns, design tokens, component props and documentation, cross-browser optimization, performance-minded HTML/CSS/JS output, Tailwind/Bootstrap/vanilla options, SVG/exportable assets, and micro-interaction suggestions. Deliverables can be isolated components, full page templates, style guides, and developer handoff-ready code. Core advantages are maintainable, testable code, strong visual hierarchy and typography, intentional color and spacing systems, and avoidance of generic AI aesthetics through bespoke visual design decisions.

SKILL.md

This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic "AI slop" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices.

The user provides frontend requirements: a component, page, application, or interface to build. They may include context about the purpose, audience, or technical constraints.

## Design Thinking [Permalink for this section](https://lobehub.com/tr/skills/chaibuilder-sdk-anthropic-frontend-design\#design-thinking)

Before coding, understand the context and commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:

- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?
- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction.
- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility).
- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?

**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work - the key is intentionality, not intensity.

Then implement working code (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Vue, etc.) that is:

- Production-grade and functional
- Visually striking and memorable
- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view
- Meticulously refined in every detail

## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines [Permalink for this section](https://lobehub.com/tr/skills/chaibuilder-sdk-anthropic-frontend-design\#frontend-aesthetics-guidelines)

Focus on:

- **Typography**: Choose fonts that are beautiful, unique, and interesting. Avoid generic fonts like Arial and Inter. Instead, opt for distinctive, unexpected, characterful font choices that elevate the frontend's aesthetics. Pair a distinctive display font with a refined body font.
- **Color & Theme**: Commit to a cohesive aesthetic. Use CSS variables for consistency. Dominant colors with sharp accents outperform timid, evenly-distributed palettes.
- **Motion**: Use animations for effects and micro-interactions. Prioritize CSS-only solutions for HTML. Use Motion library for React when available. Focus on high-impact moments: one well-orchestrated page load with staggered reveals (animation-delay) creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions. Use scroll-triggering and hover states that surprise.
- **Spatial Composition**: Unexpected layouts. Asymmetry. Overlap. Diagonal flow. Grid-breaking elements. Generous negative space OR controlled density.
- **Backgrounds & Visual Details**: Create atmosphere and depth rather than defaulting to solid colors. Add contextual effects and textures that match the overall aesthetic. Apply creative forms like gradient meshes, noise textures, geometric patterns, layered transparencies, dramatic shadows, decorative borders, custom cursors, and grain overlays.

NEVER use generic AI-generated aesthetics like overused font families (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system fonts), cliched color schemes (particularly purple gradients on white backgrounds), predictable layouts and component patterns, and cookie-cutter design that lacks context-specific character.

Interpret creatively and make unexpected choices that feel genuinely designed for the context. No design should be the same. Vary between light and dark themes, different fonts, different aesthetics. NEVER converge on common choices (Space Grotesk, for example) across generations.

**IMPORTANT**: Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision. Maximalist designs need elaborate code with extensive animations and effects. Minimalist or refined designs need restraint, precision, and careful attention to spacing, typography, and subtle details. Elegance comes from executing the vision well.

Remember: Claude is capable of extraordinary creative work. Don't hold back, show what can truly be created when thinking outside the box and committing fully to a distinctive vision.

# [Use Claude Code DESIGNER Skill to 10x UI Designs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Em9EzurouOU)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [DesignCourse](http://www.youtube.com/@DesignCourse)
**Uploaded at**: 2026-02-20T07:19:10-08:00
**Published at**: 2026-02-20T07:19:10-08:00

**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
Take the challenge #2: https://designcourse.com/app/foundations/2026-uiux
Level up your coding skills: https://scrimba.com?via=designcourse (Get 20% off)
-- Welcome to challenge #2 of the 2026 UI Design Challenege. Today, you're tasked with using Claude Code with the Frontend Designer skill to generate a hero section for a fictional business. Hundreds of designers will compete to see who can generate the best looking AI-driven design for the same, fictional, business!

https://code.claude.com/docs/en/setup
https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills
https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/plugins/frontend-design

You can participate here, and grab all of the necessary info to join the challenge:
https://designcourse.com/app/foundations/2026-uiux

On Monday, we will review these and see who made the best AI-Driven design!

#claude #ai #skills

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Subscribe for NEW VIDEOS!

Learn UI/UX:                         https://designcourse.com
My personal FB account:   http://fb.com/logodesigner
Coursetro FB:                       http://fb.com/coursetro
Coursetro's Twitter:             http://twitter.com/designcoursecom

Join my Discord!                https://discord.gg/a27CKAF
^-Chat with me and others

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Who is Gary Simon?  Well, I'm a full stack developer with 2+ decades experience and I teach people how to design and code. I've created around 100+ courses for big brands like LinkedIn, Lynda.com, Pluralsight and Envato Network. 

Now, I focus all of my time and energy on this channel and my website Designcourse.com.

Come to my discord server or add me on social media and say Hi!
```

## Transcript

I'm going to give you along with hundreds of other designers a really cool experiment. I want to see how far we can take AI design and this time is specific to Claude code to produce a hero section all on the same fictional business idea. So, currently there's an ongoing 30-day UI design challenge at designcourse.com. It's 100% free. We've already had one challenge and we've had over 200 submissions. And this is the second challenge. I'm going to show you how to use Claude Code and equip it with a special design skill in order to produce some really good designs. And the goal with this challenge is I'm going to give you all the same fictional business idea, but we're going to see how good your prompting skills are to create the best UI possible for this task. So, in a little bit, I'll show you exactly how you partake in this challenge today, no matter what time you're watching this. Could be months after this. You can always participate in this challenge to see what people are doing. But first, I'm going to show you how to set up everything you need. So, first, you're obviously going to need Claude Code. All right, so this is the page for setting it up. There's different environments. I'm not going to go through all of it. So, I'm just going to link the setup page. You can figure out how to install it if you haven't yet. And once it's ready, all you have to do is type in Claude and it should load up right here. Okay. Now, the next step is to make sure you equip it with a special skill. Now, a skill is just a set of instructions really that's fed into the context of the AI. And there's a bunch of different skills. So, before I show you the skill, this is how you install skills on your system. You have to find where your cloud code folder is based on the OS you're using and the environment that you're using. And then you're going to simply take this markdown file which is a skill and then place it into that folder. Now the particular skill I'm talking about is called front-end design right here. I will link it here in the description so that you can access it. Now what you want to do is click on skills frontend skills skill.md and then right here download raw file. All right. So once you have that skill file, you take it and you find your claude code folder in that URL that I just showed you. Now my folder here on Windows 11 happens to be crmpt users Gary andclude. Now inside of here, you may not have a skills folder. If not, create it. I already do. Double click it and then just create a folder called designer. And then inside of it, that's where you paste that skill file. Skill.md. Now, if you already have Claude running, make sure you close it first by hitting control C a few times. Type Claude again and then just type forward slashskills list all available skills. Right now, I just have three. We see right here front-end design. And the way you access this one is going to be slashfrontend design. Then you paste in your prompt. Now, let me show you this in action. So if I put forward/frontend designer designer hero section of a landing page for an AI identity verification service, it should have a light beige background with two columns, a strong headline and a copy on the left with a call to action. And on the right should be a fictional but simplistic UI animation that demonstrates the idea of identity identity verification. Sorry. Place this in an HTML file with CSS and include variables. That's all my prompt is. And this is what it ended up producing. So, I don't know about you, but this is actually really solid and it oneshotted this. And you saw the prompt. It's very small. So, I'm going to show you the challenge and the business idea of the prompt that I want you to use for the fictional business, and I want to see what you can produce. The idea is to augment it with your own design preferences. So, you can create something that's unique and even animated if you want. So the first step is to go to the designcourse.com foundations challenge. The link is below somewhere. And when you come here, you'll see this page right here. 613 students already in 197 submissions for the first challenge that we already had. This is for the second challenge. All you have to do is click here and you'll see get the 2026 UIUX Figma template. And in there is challenge number two. And that's what I want you to use. So, what that looks like is right here. This is challenge number two. And here are the instructions. This is part of the prompt that I want you to include. This is the fictional business idea. I don't want you using any other idea. I want us all to use the same idea. And that is an AI home inspection business. It uses computer vision to analyze crawl space, roof, foundation images for common defects and autogenerate standardized inspection reports. Our goal is to instruct the AI with this right here, this initial prompt in order to create a hero section that is next level with our Claude code designer skill. And over here are the instructions. All right, so use Claude code to generate a hero section for the fictional business idea featured to the right of this frame over here. So make sure you paste that in as a part of your prompt and then include the business idea, but also include your own design and styling preferences. So, that's kind of how I directed it to kind of create that little animated card that we just saw. You can use as many prompts as you want to refine the end result. You can integrate animation if you wish. And when you submit, first include the number of prompts that it took to get to the end result because I want you to refine the result if you want to. And also include the prompt itself in the comment where you can leave a comment. Now, you can also use CL the cloud code to Figma plugin to share a Figma prototype of the design in your submission, but you don't have to. Uh, a screenshot will work just fine. So, the cloud code to Figma plugin allows you to take your end result of your UI and then send it over to Figma. I'm not going to show you how to do it here because I literally just uploaded a video yesterday that shows you how to do that. Again, you don't have to do that. A screenshot would work just fine for your submission. Then when it comes to submitting your entry right here, it shows you how to do that. Let's say, for instance, if you you do decide to share a Figma document of what you're doing, which will allow me to make revisions if I do end up reviewing yours, then you just hit share over here, click copy prototype link, make sure anyone can view it, and then you submit that along with your submission. So, this is going to be a really cool challenge because we're going to see are the results going to be very similar? Well, they are. if you don't provide a lot of direction, your own creative interest in your stylings and how you infuse your creative ideas. And so if you do do that, I think we can see a lot of interesting variations and concepts that are produced. So again, specify the color scheme that you want. Specify any type of interesting illustrations or animations that you want. The goal is to try to maximize what AI can produce. All right, I hope to see everybody enter this one. This is a very cool challenge.

# Claude For Code: How to use Claude to Streamline Product Design Process

[![Nick Babich](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/1*DvFE_85Tc3fObRgRww9ZTQ.jpeg)](https://medium.com/@101?source=post_page---byline--97d4e4c43ca4---------------------------------------)

[Nick Babich](https://medium.com/@101?source=post_page---byline--97d4e4c43ca4---------------------------------------)

Follow

6 min read

·

Oct 16, 2025

545

4

[Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3D97d4e4c43ca4&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fuxplanet.org%2Fclaude-for-code-how-to-use-claude-to-streamline-product-design-process-97d4e4c43ca4&source=---header_actions--97d4e4c43ca4---------------------post_audio_button------------------)

Share

Anthropic Claude is a primary competitor of OpenAI’s ChatGPT. Just like ChatGPT this is versatile tool that can be use used in many different purposes but there is an area where Claude shines and this area is coding. Claude is great at code-adjacent design tasks and can be used as a nice supporting tool for everyday product design activities.

In this article I will explore 3 ways how UI designers can benefit from integrating this tool in their design process.

## 1\. Design-to-code translation (quick prototypes)

Once you craft a high-fidelity mockup, you need to turn it into code. Previously, you have either to wait for developers to do it or learn how to code and do it yourself. Nowadays you can simply send your design to Claude and it will code it.

I typically use Claude to generate an HTML + TailwindCSS prototype from UI specifications or UI screenshots. Here is how it works. You attach a screenshot of the UI you want to craft and let Claude do the heavy lifting for you.

_Quick note:_ I’m not saying that this appoarch will replace front-end dev work. I suggest using it only for creating prototypes for demo purposes.

For my example, I will use a screenshot of the mobile screen I’ve generated using ChatGPT 5 ( [https://uxplanet.org/ui-design-with-chatgpt-5-afc67dc501a1](https://uxplanet.org/ui-design-with-chatgpt-5-afc67dc501a1))

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*GVCd0XrqXm39q8rxoQqAqA.jpeg)

Screenshot of the mobile screen of a food delivery app I’ve generated using ChatGPT 5.

All I need to do is attach this screenshot in high resolution to Claude and ask it to generate an HTML prototype.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*PTPPN_9iWGubReX530vuAQ.png)

Initial prompt for Claude.

The moment you submit this prompt, Claude starts working on this task and writes code in real time.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*x2t8tUplOk6ljzr-9FR2VA.png)

Code output that Claude generates for me.

Note that Claude sometimes fails to show the right preview in the right-hand panel. For example, in my case, it shows something like this:

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*nxbEKjnWfC2eFpL5rPyDqA.png)

## 2\. Design system tasks

If you manage a design system for a mid or large-sized organization, you likely know that the better you create a transition between your design and code, the more chances you will achieve visual and functional consistency for your design. In other words, what you design will be implemented _precisely_ as you planned.

### Coding UI components

I find it really valuable to use Claude to convert Figma component specs (i.e., sizing, functional behavior, styling, etc.) into React components. Here is what I do to achieve this.

_Step 1:_ Create specs for a component. If you don’t have specs for your component, you can create them using plugins called Specs or Figma Exporter. These plugins generate a spec for my component. Below, I create a spec for a dropdown component used in the open-source Ant Design System ( [https://www.figma.com/community/file/822070817360747147](https://www.figma.com/community/file/822070817360747147))

## 3\. Creating micro-interaction & animation snippets

Animated effects are nice details that we add to our product to make interactions more user-friendly. Claude can help us with that by handling code tasks that will visualize interaction intent such as:

- Animate component state transitions for prototypes.
- Generate CSS keyframes for subtle hover/press effects.
- Create Framer Motion animations (fade-ins, tab transitions, button micro-interactions).
- Add easing curves and durations for consistent motion design.

## Use Anthropic Claude with HTML/CSS to Image to turn text into stunning visual assets automatically

Automate the creation of branded images from AI-generated HTML/CSS code—no design skills needed.

## Overview

Summary
